gazette: Ministry of Foreign Affairs Directorate for EU Affairs
title: New General Product Safety Regulation published in the Official Journal of the EU
bodyHtml:
"The new "General Product Safety Regulation" (GPSR), numbered (EU) 2023/988, has been prepared to update the existing rules to ensure the safety of non-food products offered in the EU market. It was published in the Official Journal of the EU on May 23, 2023.

Replacing the General Product Safety Directive 2001/95/EC, which has been in effect for over 20 years in the EU, the GPSR will enter into force on June 12, 2023, and will be fully implemented as of December 13, 2024.

The GPSR includes regulations aimed at ensuring the safety of artificial intelligence-based digital products introduced to the market with advancing technology, market surveillance and control (MSC) of products sold on digital platforms due to the increasing volume of e-commerce in recent years, and taking necessary measures against the risks posed by unsafe products imported from third countries for EU consumers.

In this context, the Regulation primarily:

-Expands the definition of "product" to include digital elements,

-Draws attention to the risks that new technologies can pose to the structure of existing products in terms of health and safety,

-Introduces the obligation to provide necessary explanations regarding the safety of products sold on digital platforms to protect consumers who purchase products through e-commerce,

-Enhances the effectiveness of the "recall" mechanism to prevent risks to consumers identified as dangerous,

-Regulates the expansion of powers of MSC authorities to effectively enforce rules on product safety,

-Imposes an obligation on manufacturers to prepare technical documentation containing risk analyses of product safety.

Click here to access the Regulation.
